text:
Two related gaps in how lume starts and orders work.

(1) NUMBERING. Iterations are numbered (001, 002, ...) but workstreams are only
slug-keyed, with no creation-order identity. There is no canonical sequence or
short handle for a workstream. Add a sequential, stable number to each
workstream alongside its slug - zero-padded to support a large corpus
(thousands; e.g. 4+ digits) - recorded in schema-validated state and surfaced in
ordered listings. Regular workstreams number from 1; the seed workstream is the
special number 0 (see below). Existing workstreams are assigned numbers by
creation order.

(2) SEED. There is no defined entry point when lume meets a repo. The first
thing should be a 'seed' workstream that builds context - the same discovery we
did for lume itself before lume ran any workflows. For a NEW/empty project, seed
is project discovery (the why/scope/constraints). For an EXISTING repo, seed is
discovery of what the repo already contains. Seed is the canonical bootstrap
workstream that turns 'lume dropped into a repo' into a tracked starting point.
Seed is a SPECIAL workstream and is always number 0 - the zeroth, pre-everything
context-building workstream; regular workstreams begin at 1.

Non-negotiables carried in: schema-validated JSON state (the number is machine
state); dependency-free (stdlib only); deterministic (no LLM in any verb/gate);
behaviour-preserving for existing workstreams (slug stays a valid handle; they
gain numbers without breaking); reuse before adding; ceremony buys its cost.

Done-when: every workstream carries a stable sequential number (padded for a
large corpus) alongside its slug, in validated state, with existing workstreams
migrated by creation order and listings ordered by it; and a seed bootstrap
exists (a verb and/or a workstream kind) that, run in a repo, starts a discovery
workstream that builds context appropriate to a new project vs an existing repo.
Concrete shapes - number width + how creation order is determined; whether the
number or slug is the primary handle; whether -w accepts a number; whether seed
is a `lume seed` verb, a workstream type, or a template; and exactly what seed
discovery captures for new-vs-existing - are discovery/planning decisions.
title: Seed Workstream & Workstream Numbering
